title: Wire options and earnings intelligence into stock signals
observation: The signal engine's get_stock_signal() already accepts options_signal_score and earnings_signal_score parameters (added in previous cycles), but the stock router never passes them. Options intelligence (get_iv_signal) and earnings intelligence (get_earnings_summary) produce signal scores that go unused. This means: (1) the only truly forward-looking data source (options-implied) is not affecting stock buy/sell signals, (2) fundamental quality (earnings beats, revenue growth) doesn't differentiate stocks, (3) signal diversity is low — 9/12 stocks show Hold with single-digit confidence.
hypothesis: Wiring options and earnings signal scores into all three stock signal paths (screener, individual analysis, signal endpoint) will increase signal differentiation and produce more actionable signals by incorporating forward-looking options data and fundamental earnings quality.
what_changed: Modified _analyze_one() in stock.py (screener) to fetch get_iv_signal() and get_earnings_summary() per ticker, passing scores to get_stock_signal()
Modified _analyze_stock() in stock.py (individual stock endpoint) with same wiring
Modified _stock_signal() in stock.py (per-stock signal endpoint) with same wiring
All three paths use try/except with graceful fallback to None (backward compatible)

Created 18 unit tests covering: options boost/reduce, earnings boost/reduce, None backward compat, clipping, reason strings, combined additive effects, opposing signal cancellation, differentiation spread, sell threshold behavior

before.signal_diversity: 2 unique actions (Buy: 3, Hold: 9), no Sell signals
before.forward_looking_data_in_signal: none (options data existed but wasn't wired in)

after.forward_looking_data_in_signal: IV skew, put/call ratio, IV rank, earnings beat rate, revenue/earnings growth, surprise trend

measured_spread.options_spread: >0.10 from bullish to bearish options (verified by test)
measured_spread.earnings_spread: >0.06 from strong to weak earnings (verified by test)

analysis: This was a straightforward but high-impact plumbing fix. The signal engine already had the parameters and logic for options/earnings — it just wasn't called. The options_iv weight (0.12) and earnings_quality weight (0.10) together add 22% influence from data sources that were previously absent. Options data is particularly valuable because it's the ONLY truly forward-looking component in the signal — everything else uses historical data. In live use, stocks with heavy put buying or bearish IV skew (like SPY's current 1.987 P/C ratio) will now pull the signal toward Sell, while stocks with strong earnings beats and bullish options flow will get a boost.
self_critique: This change adds network calls (yfinance options + earnings) to each stock in the screener, which will increase screener latency. The calls are inside the per-ticker thread so they parallelize, but each ticker now makes 2 more API calls.
The options data is only available for stocks with liquid options markets. Small-cap or low-volume stocks may return None, creating an asymmetry where large-caps get more signal inputs.
I didn't test the actual router integration with mocked yfinance calls — only tested the signal engine logic. A full integration test would require mocking yfinance responses.
The earnings data relies on yfinance earnings history which can be spotty for some tickers.
next_steps: Measure actual screener latency impact with options+earnings fetching enabled
Consider caching options/earnings signals with short TTL (5-15 min) to reduce API calls
Add options/earnings signal scores to the screener response payload so frontend can display them
Investigate whether the return_model.py (never modified by lab) could be wired into the signal engine for ML-based return predictions
depth_rating: medium — clean plumbing fix connecting existing services, not a new algorithmic contribution
impact_rating: high — unlocks forward-looking options data and fundamental earnings quality for all stock signals
